title: Add multi-step send with timing (script mode)
description:
Reduce round-trips for complex interaction sequences.

Usage:
  botty script <agent-id> <<EOF
test-edge
:sleep 500
:enter
:sleep 500
:key down
:snapshot
EOF

Or JSON/YAML format for programmatic generation.

Currently need separate botty send/sleep/send calls, which is slow and verbose for long sequences.

## Triage Notes (2026-01-29)
**Complexity**: Medium-High - new command, parser, format design, error handling
**Concerns**: Overlap with record/replay (bd-2lj), unproven need (is round-trip latency really a problem?)
**Value**: Reduces latency but unclear if this is actually painful
**Implementation**: ~6-10 hours
**Downgrade**: P3→P4 - Wait for user feedback showing round-trip latency causes real pain. May be superseded by bd-2lj.
close_reason: Closing as won't implement. This overlaps significantly with bd-2lj (record/replay). If we implement record/replay, it would generate scripts that could be replayed. If round-trip latency becomes a proven issue, the right solution is protocol-level request batching, not a script DSL. Adds API surface without demonstrated need. Will reconsider if users report round-trip latency as a real pain point.

title: Support combined wait conditions (--stable + --contains)
description:
Allow botty wait to combine stability check with pattern matching.

Current:
  botty wait id --stable 200          # OR
  botty wait id --contains "Done."

Desired:
  botty wait id --stable 200 --contains "Done."

Wait for screen to be stable (unchanged for 200ms) AND contain the pattern. Useful for waiting for prompts to fully render before proceeding.

## Triage Notes (2026-01-29)
**Complexity**: Low - Just remove clap's group="condition" constraint and change from if-else to AND logic.
**Implementation**: main.rs:642-707, ~1-2 hours
**Value**: High - Eliminates race conditions in tests, common "stable prompt" pattern
**Upgrade**: P3→P2 due to small scope + clear value
close_reason: Implemented in commit 5bbfb8e. Removed clap group constraint and changed wait condition logic from if-else to AND. Now supports combining --stable, --contains, and --pattern. Example: 'botty wait id --stable 200 --contains prompt' waits for stable AND contains. Test added, all tests pass.

title: Add resize command for agent terminal size
description: Allow resizing an agent's terminal after spawn. Add 'botty resize <id> --rows N --cols M' command. Currently rows/cols are only configurable at spawn time via --rows and --cols flags.
close_reason: Added resize command to change agent terminal size after spawn

title: Implement prompt detection heuristics
description: Detect common shell prompts ($ , > , etc.) and TUI ready states. Configurable patterns.
close_reason: Added wait_for_prompt with common shell patterns, wait_for_prompt_custom, wait_for_absence, and contains helper

title: Add tmux command palette to botty view
description:
Implement a tmux command palette for the 'botty view' session, accessible via a keybinding (e.g., Ctrl+P). This palette should offer quick access to common botty actions within the active tmux session.

**Actions to include:**
- Shutdown Botty server (`botty shutdown`)
- Detach from the current botty tmux session (`detach-client`)
- Refresh/re-layout botty panes (if such a command exists or can be simulated with `tmux refresh-client -S`)
- List currently active agents (`botty list`)
- Potentially a 'Help' entry

**Implementation details:**
- Use `tmux display-menu` to create the interactive menu.
- Bind the menu to a key in the botty-managed tmux session configuration.
- Actions should trigger relevant `botty` CLI commands or native tmux commands.
- Consider context (e.g., if the botty server isn't running, 'shutdown' should be disabled or lead to a 'server not running' message).
close_reason: Implemented in 7a6561d5. Ctrl+P opens tmux display-menu with List Agents, Snapshot Pane, Refresh Layout, Detach, and Shutdown.

title: Make kill default to SIGTERM, add --force for SIGKILL
description: Currently `kill` sends SIGKILL by default, with `--term` for SIGTERM. This is backwards — every other tool defaults to SIGTERM. Change: default to SIGTERM (graceful), add `--force`/`-f` for SIGKILL, remove `--term` flag (now redundant). The protocol already defaults to SIGTERM (signal 15); the CLI is the only place hardcoding 9. Breaking change but correct behavior.
close_reason: Implemented: kill now sends SIGTERM by default. Added --force/-f for SIGKILL. Removed --term flag.

title: Switch CLI default output format from TOON to text
description:
User feedback: TOON format uses more tokens than expected and isn't providing enough value. Switch --format text to be the default for all CLI commands that support structured output (spawn, list, kill, send, send-bytes, send-keys, recording).

Acceptance criteria:
- Default output format is text (not TOON)
- TOON format still available via --format toon
- All commands respect the new default
- Tests updated for new default

Testing strategy:
- Run each command without --format flag, verify text output
- Run each command with --format toon, verify TOON output still works
- Run existing test suite, update assertions for new default
close_reason: Duplicate of bd-28s which is already in progress by keen-otter-worker

comments.author: northern-bear-worker
comments.text: Investigation: TOON format was already removed (commit 98c76b9). Current system uses Text/Json/Pretty. Bob's concern about token usage likely refers to Pretty format being default for TTY. Investigating current behavior.

title: Validate signal numbers in kill command
description: Client-supplied signal number should be validated against allowlist (1-31) rather than silently falling back to SIGTERM.
close_reason: Added validation to reject signal numbers outside 1-31 range

title: view --new-session logs warning, messes up htop
description:
Intermittent issue: After detaching from botty view and using --new-session, sometimes see this warning:

2026-01-30T18:18:19.545788Z  WARN botty::server: Attach session error: I/O error: Broken pipe (os error 32)

The second time running view --new-session, the error doesn't appear and htop looks fine.

## Investigation needed:
- [ ] Reproduce consistently
- [ ] Determine if this is a race condition in view detach/reattach
- [ ] Check if this is related to bd-6jk (view cleanup on detach)
- [ ] Verify if it actually breaks functionality or just logs a warning

## Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Understand root cause
- [ ] Determine if this is actually a bug or just noisy logging
- [ ] Fix if it breaks functionality, or suppress warning if benign
close_reason: Fixed in commit 59175309. Root cause identified: tmux kill-session abruptly terminates attach processes, causing expected broken pipe errors. Fixed by downgrading broken pipe warnings to debug level. No functional issue - htop and other TUI programs work fine. Investigation documented in commit message.

title: Propagate exit code from exec command
description: exec currently always returns 0 even if the command failed. Should capture and propagate the actual exit code so agents can detect failures.
close_reason: Implemented in commit e9e8dd4

title: Implement snapshot command
description: Return normalized screen text from vt100 parser. Strip ANSI colors by default. Support --raw for full cell attributes.
close_reason: Implemented snapshot command with --raw flag for ANSI codes

title: Implement attach command (TTY bridging)
description: Bridge user terminal to agent PTY. Put user terminal in raw mode. Forward input/output bidirectionally. Handle window resize (SIGWINCH).
close_reason: Implemented attach mode with Ctrl+A d detach sequence and readonly option

title: when all agents killed at once, botty view is killed
description: it shouldn't be

title: botty-list tmux alias should use --format text
description: The botty-list tmux command alias (used in display-popup) should use --format text for cleaner output in the popup. Depends on --format support being added first.
close_reason: botty-list tmux alias now uses --format text for columnar output in popups.

title: Fix auto-resize: wrong sizes and no dynamic resizing
description:
See docs/auto-resize-fix-notes.md for detailed progress notes.

## Summary

Fixed auto-resize in botty view by switching from `tail --follow --raw` to `attach --readonly`:

1. **Server sends initial screen render** - After AttachStarted, renders full screen state
2. **Attach handles SIGWINCH** - Forwards resize events to server, agent redraws
3. **Auto-resize on by default** - Changed from --auto-resize flag to --no-resize

## Acceptance criteria:
- [x] Agents resize to correct pane dimensions on view
- [x] Agents resize dynamically when tmux panes are resized
- [x] Works in both panes and windows modes
- [x] Clean display for TUI programs after resize
close_reason: Fixed by switching to attach --readonly with server-side initial screen render and SIGWINCH handling

title: view reattach: ensure remain-on-exit is set
description: The reattach path in run_view_command (src/main.rs:2002) skips create_session() and never re-sets remain-on-exit on the window. If the session was created by an older botty version or the option got cleared, the last pane process exiting destroys the session. Fix: in the reattach path, ensure remain-on-exit is set on the agents window, same as create_session does.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.13.0. Reattach path now calls ensure_remain_on_exit().

title: Consider renaming 'agent' to 'pty' or 'session' in data model
description:
The tool was built for spawning agents, but it's really a PTY manager that could be used for anything (TUI development, terminal multiplexing, screen capture).

Current naming ('agent') is confusing when spawning non-agent programs like htop.

Options:
- `pty` - technically accurate, jargon-y
- `session` - tmux-like, generic
- `terminal` / `term` - user-friendly

Would affect:
- CLI output ('agents' → 'sessions' or 'ptys')
- Protocol: SpawnAgent → Spawn, AgentId → SessionId/PtyId
- Code: struct Agent → struct Session/Pty
- Docs, error messages, help text

Note: The name 'botty' itself implies bots/agents but could just be quirky branding.
close_reason: Deferred: Large refactor affecting entire codebase (CLI, protocol, code, docs). Current naming 'agent' is acceptable and consistent. Not worth the churn unless project scope expands significantly. Would revisit if botty becomes a general-purpose terminal multiplexer.

title: Add --no-resize flag to spawn command
description:
Add a --no-resize flag to botty spawn that marks an agent as resize-immune. When view's auto-resize runs (resize_agents_to_panes), it skips agents with this flag. This prevents view from corrupting snapshot dimensions for agents that scripts/other processes are reading programmatically.

Implementation:
- Add no_resize: bool to Agent struct
- Add --no-resize to spawn CLI and Request::Spawn protocol
- Pass through server handler to Agent::new
- In resize_agents_to_panes (main.rs ~2071), skip agents where no_resize is true
- Include no_resize in AgentInfo so list output shows it

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] botty spawn --no-resize -- bash starts agent with resize lock
- [ ] botty view auto-resize skips no-resize agents
- [ ] botty resize <id> still works (explicit resize is allowed)
- [ ] botty list shows no-resize status
- [ ] All existing tests pass
close_reason: Implemented --no-resize flag on spawn. Agents with this flag are skipped by view's auto-resize (both initial and tmux hook paths). Explicit botty resize still works. Flag shown in list JSON output.

title: wait --exited: support multiple agent IDs
description: Change wait command to accept multiple agent IDs (id: Vec<String>) so you can do 'botty wait --exited agent1 agent2 agent3'. Subscribe to events for all specified agents, track which have exited, break when all are done. Exit code: highest non-zero from any agent. Only allow multiple IDs with --exited; error if combined with screen-based conditions (--contains, --pattern, --stable) since those are inherently single-agent. Single-ID usage stays unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented in v0.13.0. wait --exited now accepts multiple agent IDs.

title: Fix --replay missing newline between snapshot and stream
description: When using 'botty tail --replay', the snapshot output doesn't include a trailing newline, causing the first streamed line to concatenate with the last snapshot line.
close_reason: Added newline after snapshot if content doesn't end with one

title: Design and implement agent orchestration test framework
description: Create a testing framework for the primary use case: one coding agent spawning and coordinating multiple TUI agents. Need to handle snapshot-based assertions, timing, and realistic multi-agent scenarios.
close_reason: Completed test framework with TestHarness, AgentHandle, wait_for_* methods, prompt detection, and 7 orchestration scenarios

title: view --new-session breaks htop UI
description:
When running botty view --new-session after spawning htop, the UI is broken with missing elements.

Reproduction:
1. botty view
2. botty spawn -- htop
3. botty view --new-session
4. Result: htop UI has missing elements all over the place

This might be related to:
- How --new-session kills and recreates the tmux session
- The broken pipe fix we just made (bd-1ax)
- Screen/terminal state not being properly reset
- PTY state corruption when panes are forcibly killed

Need to investigate what's happening to the htop agent's PTY when the tmux session is killed and recreated.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 6d402b31. Root cause: attach didn't send initial resize, only on SIGWINCH. When view --new-session recreated panes at different sizes, PTYs were out of sync with terminals, causing garbled TUI rendering. Fix: send initial resize request to sync PTY with terminal dimensions on attach start.

comments.author: Bob
comments.text: User deprioritized 2026-01-31: Multiple fix attempts (SIGWINCH on clear, skip stale screen render) didn't resolve the issue reliably. Leaving open but deprioritized for now.

title: Support unique tmux session names per view instance
description:
Allow multiple botty view instances to run simultaneously without interfering.

## Problem
The tmux session name is hardcoded as 'botty', which means:
- Can't run multiple view instances
- Multiple users on same machine would conflict
- Reattaching might grab wrong session

## Proposed solutions
1. Generate unique session names (e.g., 'botty-<pid>' or 'botty-<uuid>')
2. Add --session flag to specify custom name
3. Both: generate by default, allow override

## Implementation notes
From security review cr-klwd (th-grfz). Low security impact, mainly a usability issue.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Multiple `botty view` instances can run simultaneously
- [ ] Each gets unique tmux session name
- [ ] Session names are discoverable (maybe `botty view --list`?)
- [ ] Can specify custom session name with `--session`
- [ ] Default naming is predictable (e.g., botty-<pid>)

## Labels
view, tmux, enhancement

title: Server skeleton with Unix socket listener
description: Create server module with tokio-based Unix socket listener, handle incoming connections, dispatch to request handlers. Include auto-fork/daemonize logic.
close_reason: Implemented server with Unix socket listener, request handling, PTY output polling

title: shutdown should kill the botty tmux session
description:
When `botty shutdown` is run, it stops the server but leaves the tmux session ("botty") running. The tmux session should be killed as part of shutdown to avoid orphaned sessions.

Note: The detach handler (main.rs:1789-1798) already does this when no agents are running after detach. The shutdown command should do the same unconditionally.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `botty shutdown` kills the tmux session in addition to stopping the server
- [ ] Works even if agents are still running (shutdown is intentional teardown)
- [ ] No error if tmux session does not exist (idempotent)

## Testing
- `botty view && botty spawn -- bash && botty shutdown` — verify no orphaned tmux session
- `botty shutdown` without view running — should not error
- Check `tmux ls` after shutdown confirms session is gone
close_reason: Merged. Shutdown now kills tmux session.

comments.author: botty-dev
comments.text: Groomed: expanded description with implementation pointer (detach handler already does tmux cleanup — shutdown should reuse that pattern), added acceptance criteria and testing strategy, added cli label

title: Use SIGKILL as default for kill command
description: Interactive shells (bash) ignore SIGTERM, requiring users to remember -9. Change default to SIGKILL for reliable cleanup. Add --term flag for graceful SIGTERM when needed.
close_reason: Implemented in commit defabd5

title: botty view reattach: reuse existing tmux session instead of recreating
description:
When running botty view, detaching, then running botty view again, agents can appear in a garbled state. This is accompanied by:

  WARN botty::server: Attach session error: I/O error: Broken pipe (os error 32)

Root cause: botty view currently kills the existing tmux session and creates a fresh one every time. This tears down all the readonly attach connections (causing broken pipe errors) and re-establishes them, which can leave the screen in a garbled state during the transition.

Fix: botty view should check if a botty tmux session already exists. If it does, just reattach to it (tmux attach-session). Only create a new session if one doesn't exist. The event loop already handles adding/removing panes dynamically, so the existing session should stay up to date.
close_reason: Fixed: botty view now reattaches to existing tmux session instead of destroying/recreating. Added --new-session flag to force fresh session when needed.

title: Consider explicit protocol framing for attach
description:
From security review cr-qmr8 (th-r7r3):

## Original Report
[MEDIUM] Protocol framing ambiguity. The code mixes JSON messages and raw binary PTY data on the same connection, relying on timing-based delimiters to distinguish them. This is fragile and could lead to protocol confusion attacks where PTY output is misinterpreted as control messages.

## Current Decision: Won't Fix

Declined because:

1. **Local IPC only** - There's no network attacker. The only parties are the client and server, both running as you.

2. **Direction matters** - The "confusion" would be PTY output (from agent → client) being misinterpreted as server control messages. But PTY output flows in one direction after attach starts—there's no parsing of it as JSON.

3. **In practice it works** - The initial JSON response is read before entering the raw I/O bridge. After that, everything is just PTY data forwarded to stdout.

4. **Proper fix is expensive** - Length-prefixed framing or out-of-band signaling would require protocol changes, version negotiation, and client/server coordination. Overkill for a local tool.

## If Revisited
If botty needs bidirectional control messages during attach (e.g., server-initiated disconnect, bandwidth throttling), proper framing would be needed. Options:
- Length-prefixed binary framing (like HTTP/2)
- Magic byte prefix for control messages (e.g., 0x00 + length + JSON)
- Separate control socket
close_reason: Won't fix: Protocol framing ambiguity is acceptable for local IPC tool. Documented in security review cr-qmr8. Would only revisit if botty needs remote connections or bidirectional control messages during attach.

title: Update remaining commands to use OutputFormat conventions
description:
Update spawn, kill, send, snapshot, dump, recording, and gen-test to follow output conventions.

## Commands to update
- **spawn**: Output agent ID (text: just ID, json: `{ "agent": { "id": "...", "pid": N }, "advice": ["botty send <id> ..."] }`)
- **kill/send**: Consistent success/error output with next-step advice
- **snapshot**: Align format options (text/json/pretty instead of raw/jsonl)
- **dump**: Align format options
- **recording**: Already outputs JSON; add text format option
- **doctor**: Already close; minor tweaks if needed

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All mutation commands support --format text/json (pretty where it makes sense)
- [ ] JSON responses always have advice[] field
- [ ] Text output is ID-first with suggested next commands
- [ ] No command uses bare println for structured output

## Testing
- Build and test each command
- Verify JSON output with jq for all commands that support it
close_reason: Merged. Spawn/kill/send/recording commands now support --format with text/json/pretty.

title: Add signal subcommand for sending arbitrary signals to agents
description: Add a `signal` subcommand that sends arbitrary Unix signals to agent processes. Usage: `botty signal <agent> <signal>` where signal is a name (SIGUSR1, SIGHUP, SIGINT) or number. This is the low-level escape hatch; `kill` remains the high-level stop command. Supports --label, --all, --proc filters like kill does.
close_reason: Implemented: botty signal <agent> --signal <SIG> sends arbitrary Unix signals. Supports names (TERM, USR1, HUP, etc.) and numbers. Case-insensitive, SIG prefix optional.

title: Consider moving SIGWINCH signaling server-side
description:
From security review cr-qmr8 (th-bepr):

## Original Report
[CRITICAL] Unsafe signal sending to arbitrary PIDs. The server returns a PID which the client trusts and sends SIGWINCH to. A malicious or compromised server could return any PID, causing signals to be sent to unrelated processes. Additionally, PID reuse attacks are possible - if the original process dies and another process takes its PID, you'll signal the wrong process.

## Current Decision: Won't Fix

Declined because:

1. **Threat model mismatch** - botty is local IPC over a Unix socket. The server runs as the same user, on the same machine. If an attacker can compromise the server or MITM the socket, they already have code execution as your user—sending SIGWINCH to a process is the least of your problems.

2. **We control the PID source** - The PID comes from our own spawn operation. The server tracks which PIDs it spawned. There's no external input.

3. **SIGWINCH is benign** - Even if somehow the wrong process received it, SIGWINCH just tells a process "your terminal resized." Most processes ignore it entirely. It can't cause privilege escalation or data corruption.

4. **Fix would add complexity** - Moving signaling server-side means the server needs to track which attach clients want resize notifications, adding state and message types for minimal security benefit.

## If Revisited
If botty ever supports remote connections or multi-user scenarios, this should be reconsidered. The fix would be to add a Request::SendSignal { agent_id, signal } that the server validates before forwarding.
close_reason: Won't fix: SIGWINCH client-side signaling is acceptable for local tool with same-user threat model. Server tracks PIDs it spawned, SIGWINCH is benign. Documented in security review cr-qmr8. Would only revisit for remote/multi-user scenarios.

title: Protocol types (Request/Response enums)
description: Define all message types for client-server IPC: Spawn, List, Kill, Send, SendBytes, Tail, Dump, Snapshot, Attach, Shutdown requests and corresponding responses
close_reason: Implemented Request/Response enums with serde serialization and unit tests

title: Add integration tests for attach mode
description: Attach mode has zero test coverage. Need tests for: attach/detach flow, detach key sequence, read-only mode, agent exit during attach.
close_reason: Added 5 integration tests for attach mode: attach/detach, readonly, nonexistent agent, output reception, agent exit

title: Add assertion commands for testing
description:
Add built-in assertion commands to make test failures clearer and more idiomatic.

## Problem
Currently using `botty snapshot | grep -q ...` which:
- Gives poor error messages on failure
- Requires shell pipes and external tools
- Doesn't show what was actually found vs expected

## Proposed solution
```bash
botty assert <agent-id> --contains "Done." --timeout 10
botty assert <agent-id> --pattern "\d+ tasks completed"
botty assert <agent-id> --not-contains "Error"
```

## Behavior
- Exit code 0 if assertion passes
- Exit code 1 if assertion fails
- Print clear error message showing expected vs actual on failure
- Support timeout (like wait command)

## Implementation notes
Similar to existing wait command (main.rs:642-707). Can reuse polling logic.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `botty assert <id> --contains <text>` passes if text in output
- [ ] `botty assert <id> --not-contains <text>` passes if text NOT in output
- [ ] `botty assert <id> --pattern <regex>` passes if regex matches
- [ ] `--timeout <seconds>` waits for condition (default: fail immediately)
- [ ] Clear error message on failure showing what was expected vs found
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure

## Labels
testing, cli
close_reason: Completed in commit eaa31d26. Added assert command with --contains, --not-contains, --pattern flags. Clear error messages on failure showing expected vs actual. Exit code 0 on pass, 1 on fail.

title: Attach mode drops input after typing a few characters
description: Input to attached terminal is inconsistent - works fine initially but progressively drops more input after typing a few characters. Likely a bug in the I/O bridge or buffering logic.
close_reason: Fixed: background pty_reader_task was competing with attach bridge for PTY reads. Added 'attached' flag to skip agents during attach.

title: Structured agent messaging protocol
description: Agents can emit __BOTTY_MSG:{json}__ markers. Orchestrator retrieves via 'botty recv <id>'. Enables agents to communicate structured data back.
close_reason: Won't implement - botbus provides this functionality already (send, history, watch, inbox). Explicit botbus calls are cleaner than parsing markers from stdout.

title: Change list to show only running agents by default, add --all flag
description: botty list should only show running agents. Add --all to include exited ones. Keeps the list clean for active orchestration.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 5b6b3b1

title: Implement tail --follow for streaming output
description: The --follow flag exists but isn't implemented. Should stream new output as it arrives, essential for watching long-running commands.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 41400ee

title: Add TOON output format for list command
description: Default to TOON format for list output (token-efficient for LLMs). Add --json flag for piping to jq. Use the toon-format crate (official implementation) for encoding. TOON is ideal for tabular agent data.
close_reason: Implemented in commit e9c7bab

title: Implement send command (UTF-8 input)
description: Write UTF-8 text to agent PTY. Support --newline flag to auto-append \n.
close_reason: Implemented send command with --no-newline flag

title: Allow slashes in agent names for parent/child convention
description:
Allow `/` in agent names so agents spawning subagents can use a hierarchical naming convention like `parent/child`.

## Current behavior
Agent name validation (server/mod.rs:348) only allows `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`. Slashes are rejected.

## Proposed change
Add `/` to the allowed character set. Constraints:
- No leading/trailing slashes
- No double slashes (`//`)
- Max one level of nesting may be sufficient (TBD)
- Ensure slash does not cause issues with any path-based lookups or shell escaping

## Convention
```bash
# Parent agent spawns children
botty spawn --name myagent/researcher -- ...
botty spawn --name myagent/implementer -- ...

# List shows hierarchy
botty list  # myagent/researcher, myagent/implementer
```

## Consideration
Evaluate whether slash needs special handling in any existing code paths (event routing, tmux pane naming, file paths, shell completion).
close_reason: Already released

title: Keep server alive for persistent view mode
description:
Currently the server shuts down when no PTYs are active, which kills the tmux session and kicks you out of botty view.

Desired behavior:
1. Run `botty view`
2. Opens tmux session with 'waiting for agents...' placeholder
3. Autonomous agent runs `botty spawn` → PTY appears in tmux
4. PTY terminates → goes back to 'waiting for agents...'
5. Repeat - view stays open as a persistent dashboard

This enables using botty view as a monitoring dashboard for agent orchestration, where agents spawn and terminate over time.

Implementation considerations:
- Server needs to stay alive when PTY count goes to zero
- View mode needs a placeholder pane/message when no PTYs exist
- May want different behavior for `view` (persistent) vs other commands (ephemeral)
- Consider `--keep-alive` flag or make it default for view mode
close_reason: Implemented in v0.3.0 - persistent view mode with waiting placeholder

title: Switch CLI default output format from toon to text
description:
The CLI's structured outputs currently use TOON format by default. This uses more tokens than expected without clear value. Switch --format text as the default for structured outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Commands that currently default to TOON format should default to text format
- --format toon flag should still work for users who want it
- All CLI commands with structured output are updated

Testing:
- Verify commands output text format by default
- Verify --format toon still works
- Run cargo test to ensure no regressions

title: View shows panes for killed agents
description: After killing an agent, 'botty view' still shows a pane for it. The view command should only show running agents, and the event loop should properly remove panes when agents exit.
close_reason: Kill and recreate tmux session on view to avoid stale panes

title: Add --name flag to spawn for custom agent IDs
description: Allow botty spawn --name my-worker -- bash. Makes it easier to refer to agents by role rather than random names.
close_reason: Implemented in commit f4256f0

title: CLI with clap (command definitions)
description: Create CLI using clap derive macros: spawn, list, kill, send, send-bytes, tail, dump, snapshot, attach, server subcommands. Wire up to client calls.
close_reason: Implemented CLI with clap: spawn, list, kill, send, send-bytes, tail, dump, snapshot, attach, server, shutdown

title: Fix clippy: add #[must_use] attributes
description: Add #[must_use] attributes to ~27 functions that return values which shouldn't be silently discarded
close_reason: Fixed all clippy lints

title: Create TestHarness struct for managing server lifecycle
description: A test harness that handles server startup, client creation, and cleanup. Should support unique socket paths per test.
close_reason: Implemented TestHarness, AgentHandle, wait_for_content, wait_for_pattern, and wait_for_stable in testing module

title: Update botty list to use OutputFormat and JSON envelope
description:
Migrate `botty list` from toon/json/text to the new OutputFormat system.

## Changes
- Replace --format toon/json/text with text/json/pretty
- Drop toon_format dependency from this command
- JSON output: wrap in `{ "agents": [...], "advice": ["botty kill <id>", ...] }`
- Text output: ID-first, two-space delimiters, no header row, suggested next command
- Pretty output: colored table like current text but with formatting
- Remove deprecated --json flag

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `botty list --format json` returns envelope with agents[] and advice[]
- [ ] `botty list --format text` returns ID-first compact records
- [ ] `botty list --format pretty` returns colored human-friendly table
- [ ] Default is TTY-detected (pretty for terminal, text for pipe)
- [ ] --json deprecated flag still works (maps to --format json)

## Testing
- Build and run `botty list` in both TTY and piped contexts
- Verify JSON envelope structure with jq
- Verify text output is grep-friendly
close_reason: Merged. List command migrated to text/json/pretty with JSON envelope.

title: Fix FD lifetime issue in attach bridge - potential use-after-free
description: In run_attach_bridge(), pty_fd is captured at attach start but the agent could be removed while the bridge is running. After drop(mgr), the fd may become invalid on next iteration. Need Arc<OwnedFd> or verify agent exists after each lock acquisition.
close_reason: Fixed by always acquiring lock before using PTY fd, never passing raw fd across async boundaries

title: View event loop doesn't track existing panes
description: The event loop in run_view_event_loop creates a fresh TmuxView with empty active_panes. This causes: (1) new agents to replace existing panes instead of adding, (2) session to close when any agent exits because is_empty() returns true. Need to initialize event loop with existing agent IDs.
close_reason: Pass existing agent IDs to event loop and initialize tracking with mark_pane_exists()

title: Fix clippy: add const fn where possible
description: Add const to ~12 functions that can be evaluated at compile time
close_reason: Fixed all clippy lints

title: Add record/replay mode for test authoring
description:
Epic: Record manual sessions and generate test scripts.

## Vision
Dramatically speed up test authoring by recording manual interactions and generating executable test scripts.

## Breakdown
This epic has been split into:
1. **bd-3r3**: Add basic command recording - Track commands sent to agents
2. **bd-3l8**: Generate test scripts from recordings - Convert logs to bash scripts
3. **Future**: Replay mode (not yet created) - Execute recorded scripts

## Current Status
Start with bd-3r3 to prove the recording infrastructure, then move to bd-3l8 for script generation.

## Original vision
```bash
botty record <agent-id>
# ... manually interact ...
botty stop-recording <agent-id> > test-script.sh
```

Optional replay:
```bash
botty replay <agent-id> test-script.sh
```

## Note
Consider closing this epic once subtasks are complete, or keep it open to track the overall feature.
close_reason: Epic complete. Recording (bd-3r3) and script generation (bd-3l8) both merged.

title: tail -n is ignored: server returns full transcript
description: The server handler for Request::Tail (src/server/mod.rs:569) ignores the lines parameter entirely (lines: _) and always returns agent.transcript.all_bytes(). The transcript has tail_bytes(n) but it works on byte count not line count. Need to: 1) convert lines to a byte-based tail (or add a tail_lines method to transcript), 2) use it in the server handler instead of all_bytes(). The follow-mode path in main.rs also uses Request::Tail but needs the full transcript for offset tracking, so lines should only limit the initial output in one-shot mode.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.13.1. Server now respects the lines parameter via new tail_lines() method. Follow mode passes lines=0 for full transcript offset tracking.

title: Output subscriptions: botty subscribe
description: Stream output from one or more agents as it arrives. Multiplexed streaming for watching multiple workers from one coordinator.
close_reason: Implemented botty subscribe command for streaming agent output

title: Agent struct and state management
description: Define Agent struct: id (adjective-noun), pid, pty_fd, state enum (Running/Stopped/Exited), exit_code. Use names crate for ID generation with collision handling.
close_reason: Implemented Agent struct with id, command, pty, state, transcript, screen

title: Implement tail command
description: Read last N lines from transcript. Support --follow mode for streaming new output.
close_reason: Implemented tail command (follow mode pending)

title: Add named key sequences to botty send
description:
Instead of hex codes like 'botty send-bytes id "1b5b42"' for arrow keys, support named sequences: 'botty send-key id down' or 'botty send id --key down'.

Common keys to support: up, down, left, right, enter, tab, escape, ctrl-c, ctrl-d, backspace, delete, home, end, pageup, pagedown.

Makes test scripts much more readable. Fall back to hex for exotic sequences.

Example:
botty send-key test-agent down
botty send-key test-agent enter
botty send-key test-agent ctrl-c

## Triage Notes (2026-01-29)
**Complexity**: Low-Medium - Extend parse_key_notation() (cli.rs:6-47) to return Vec<u8>, add ANSI sequence lookup table
**Current**: Already handles ctrl-X, ^X, single chars for detach key
**Implementation**: Create send-key subcommand or --key flag, reuse SendBytes protocol, ~2-4 hours
**Value**: High - Common use case, dramatically improves test readability
**Keep**: P2 (high value, low risk, isolated change)
close_reason: Implemented in commit e231efe. Added 'botty send-key' command with support for arrow keys, special keys, navigation keys, control sequences, and function keys. Uses existing SendBytes protocol. Comprehensive tests added. All tests pass.

title: Implement wait_for_content with polling and timeout
description: Poll snapshot until content matches (substring or regex), with configurable timeout. Replaces brittle sleep-based waits.
close_reason: Implemented TestHarness, AgentHandle, wait_for_content, wait_for_pattern, and wait_for_stable in testing module

title: Virtual screen model with vt100 crate
description: Integrate vt100 crate for VT/ANSI screen parsing. Feed PTY output to parser. Track rows x cols, cursor position, alt-screen state.
close_reason: Implemented Screen struct with vt100 parser, process, snapshot, cursor_position methods

title: Add --cwd flag to spawn command
description: Spawned processes inherit the botty server's working directory, not the client's. Add a --cwd option to spawn that sets the child process working directory before execvp. This allows commands with relative paths to work correctly regardless of where the server daemon is running from.
close_reason: Implemented --cwd flag for spawn command. Adds chdir in PTY child process before execvp.

title: Create multi-agent orchestration test scenarios
description: Write example tests demonstrating: spawn multiple agents, coordinate between them, assert on each agent's state.
close_reason: Created 7 orchestration test scenarios covering: multi-worker spawning, agent pipelines, stateful agents, failure isolation, rapid spawn/kill, concurrent commands, TUI screen updates

title: Auto-resize agents to match tmux pane size
description: When viewing agents in tmux, resize each agent's PTY to match its tmux pane dimensions. Could use tmux hooks (pane-resized) or periodic polling to detect size changes and call 'botty resize' accordingly. Need to investigate feasibility.
close_reason: Added auto-resize of agents to match tmux pane sizes when viewing

title: view event loop: bail out on dead tmux session
description: When the tmux session dies (e.g. killed externally, race on last pane), the event loop in run_view_event_loop (src/main.rs:2110) keeps processing queued events and spewing 'can't find session: botty' warnings for every add_pane/remove_pane call. Fix: detect session-not-found errors from tmux operations and break out of the event loop instead of continuing.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.13.0. Event loop checks session_exists() after tmux errors and breaks out.

title: Allow reusing names of exited agents
description: Currently, spawning an agent with a name that exists (even if exited) fails with 'agent name already in use'. Should allow reusing names of exited agents - either by auto-removing them or by checking state during spawn.
close_reason: Allow reusing names by removing exited agents before spawn

title: Implement spawn command end-to-end
description: Wire up spawn: CLI -> client -> server -> PTY creation -> agent registration. Return agent ID to client. Verify with list command.
close_reason: Implemented as part of CLI and server work - spawn works end-to-end

title: Cursor flashing in `botty view`
description: Not sure if is botty thing or a terminal thing, but in apps that are doing continuous full-screen redraws I can see the cursor flashing continuously between 2 positions. See notes/cursor-flashing.png for a screenshot.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 58cbfcaf. Hidden cursor in readonly attach mode using DECTCEM sequences (ESC[?25l/h).

title: Tmux resize hook errors shown in other sessions
description:
When running botty view, the tmux resize hooks trigger in other tmux sessions too, showing error messages like:

```
'./target/release/botty resize-panes --mode=panes' returned 1
'/home/bob/src/botty/target/release/botty resize-panes --mode=panes' returned 1
```

User has to press 'q' to dismiss.

The hook is probably being set at the server level instead of just the botty session, or the resize-panes command is failing when called from outside the botty session context.

Should either:
1. Scope the hook to only the botty tmux session
2. Make resize-panes fail silently when not in botty context
3. Suppress tmux error display for the hook
close_reason: Fixed in commit c2e1904. Made resize-panes exit with code 0 even on errors (socket connection failures, etc). Since it's called from tmux hooks, failures are non-critical and we just log them as warnings instead of showing error popups to users. Tested and working.

title: Client module for Unix socket IPC
description: Create client module: connect to socket, send Request, receive Response. Include auto-start logic (fork server on ENOENT/ECONNREFUSED).
close_reason: Implemented client with auto-connect, server auto-start, request/response handling

title: Configurable detach sequence for attach mode
description: Allow users to configure the detach key sequence via CLI flag or config file. Currently hardcoded to Ctrl+G d.
close_reason: Added --detach-key flag with support for ctrl-X, ^X, and single char notation

title: change send-key to send-keys, allow multiple separated by space
close_reason: Completed in commit 6920fb50. Renamed send-key to send-keys and added support for sending multiple key sequences in a single command invocation.

title: Read-only attach mode
description: Support --readonly flag for attach: forward output to user but discard input (except detach sequence).
close_reason: Implemented attach mode with Ctrl+A d detach sequence and readonly option

title: Event streaming: botty events
description: Stream JSON events for agent lifecycle (spawned, output, exited). Enables reactive orchestration without polling. This is the key differentiator from tmux.
close_reason: Implemented event streaming with botty events command

title: Adopt CLI output conventions: JSON envelope, drop TOON, add pretty format
description:
Epic: Adopt CLI output conventions from botbox v0.5.0 across all botty commands.

See .agents/botbox/design/cli-conventions.md for the full spec.

## Key changes
1. **Format infrastructure**: Shared OutputFormat enum (text/json/pretty) with TTY auto-detection (--format flag > FORMAT env > TTY→pretty / non-TTY→text)
2. **JSON envelope**: All JSON output wrapped in named objects with advice[] field
3. **Text format**: ID-first records, two-space delimiters, no prose, suggested next commands
4. **Pretty format**: Colored, human-friendly output for TTY (replaces current default)
5. **Drop toon**: Remove toon_format dependency; text is sufficient for agents

## Commands to update
- `botty list` — currently has toon/json/text; needs json envelope, pretty default
- `botty dump` — currently text/jsonl
- `botty snapshot` — currently raw/jsonl
- `botty spawn/kill/send/etc` — ad-hoc println, needs consistent text + json output
- `botty doctor` — already close to conventions
- `botty recording/gen-test` — new commands, should adopt from the start

## Broken down into subtasks (see dependencies).
close_reason: Epic complete. All subtasks merged: OutputFormat module (bd-zzz), list migration (bd-2el), other commands (bd-1w6), toon removal (bd-m4k).

comments.author: botty-dev
comments.text: Groomed: converted to epic, broken into 4 subtasks with dependency chain. bd-zzz (format module) → bd-2el (list) + bd-1w6 (other commands) → bd-m4k (remove toon) → bd-378 (epic closed).

title: Add symlink check for socket path to prevent /tmp attacks
description: Socket path in /tmp could be symlinked by attacker. Check for symlink before remove/bind, and prefer XDG_RUNTIME_DIR.
close_reason: Added socket permission 0o600 and symlink check in server startup

title: Implement dump command
description: Export transcript with options: --since timestamp, --format text|jsonl. JSONL includes timestamps per chunk.
close_reason: Implemented dump command with --since and --format options

title: TUIs don't update in view/tail --replay
description: When viewing TUI programs like htop/btop via 'botty view' (which uses tail --replay), the initial screen shows correctly but updates don't render properly. The snapshot outputs rendered text without cursor positioning, then streaming raw escape codes don't align correctly. Need to either: (A) stream periodic snapshots, or (B) stream raw transcript from start and let TUI codes rebuild screen.
close_reason: Fixed by returning full transcript from Tail instead of truncated 4KB chunk. Replay now dumps full transcript and streaming compares correctly.

title: Add --raw flag to snapshot for ANSI output
description: snapshot --raw flag exists but has bugs - the vt100 rows_formatted() output includes cursor positioning codes that cause garbled display. Need to investigate vt100 API more carefully or use a different approach. Currently the flag is broken.
close_reason: Fixed by implementing proper cell-by-cell iteration to emit ANSI codes without cursor positioning sequences

title: Change 'No running agents' message to avoid grep confusion
description: The message 'No running agents' contains the word 'running' which causes naive grep-based checks to match. Change to 'No agents currently running' or similar.
close_reason: Implemented in commit e9c7bab

title: Spawned agents don't inherit client environment variables
description:
## Problem
When spawning agents without `--env`, environment variables set in the calling shell are not inherited by the spawned agent.

## Reproduction
```bash
export TEST_VAR="hello"
botty spawn --name test -- sh -c 'echo TEST_VAR=$TEST_VAR'
botty snapshot test
# Shows: TEST_VAR=  (empty)
```

## Root Cause
The botty server is started as a separate background process when first needed. Spawned agents inherit the server's environment (captured at server startup time), not the client's current environment.

This is because:
1. Client connects to server via Unix socket
2. `Request::Spawn` is sent over socket
3. Server forks to create agent
4. Agent inherits server's env, not client's env

## Impact
- Blocking botbox workflows that expect env inheritance
- Users must use explicit `--env VAR=value` for every variable they need
- Counter-intuitive behavior vs. direct process spawning

## Options
1. **Transmit client env in Spawn request** (adds bandwidth, privacy/security considerations)
2. **Selective inheritance** - transmit specific vars (BOTBUS_AGENT, EDITOR, etc.)
3. **Document as limitation** - require explicit --env (current behavior)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Spawned agents see env vars from calling client
- [ ] Security: don't transmit sensitive vars by default (or make opt-in)
- [ ] Document behavior

## Labels
spawn, environment, bug, blocking
close_reason: Resolved: agents inherit server env (not client env) by design. Explicit --env KEY=VALUE flag provides secure selective inheritance. No env vars pass through by default.

comments.author: botty-dev
comments.text: Bob suggested --pass-env VAR_1,VAR_2,VAR_3 approach for security. Implementing selective env inheritance via explicit flag.

title: Set explicit Unix socket permissions (0o700)
description: Socket permissions are determined by umask, which could allow other users to connect. Set explicit 0o700 permissions after binding.
close_reason: Added socket permission 0o600 and symlink check in server startup

title: Implement send-bytes command (raw input)
description: Write raw bytes to agent PTY. Accept hex-encoded or base64 input for control characters, escape sequences, etc.
close_reason: Implemented send-bytes command with hex-encoded input

title: Add --exited flag to botty wait
description:
Add --exited flag to `botty wait` that blocks until an agent exits, then returns the agent's exit code as the process exit code.

## Use case
Agents without subagent capabilities can use botty to spawn subagents, wait for them, then collect output:

```bash
botty spawn --name sub1 -- do-thing-1
botty spawn --name sub2 -- do-thing-2

# wait + collect (sequential)
botty wait --exited sub1 && botty dump sub1
botty wait --exited sub2 && botty dump sub2

# or parallel
botty wait --exited sub1 & botty wait --exited sub2 & wait
botty dump sub1; botty dump sub2
```

## Behavior
- If agent already exited: return immediately with its exit code
- If agent is running: block until exit (subject to --timeout)
- Exit code propagation: `botty wait --exited` exits with the agent's exit code
- Composable with --print to dump final screen on exit
- Show the above example in --help (after_help or long_about)

## Implementation notes
- Add `--exited` bool flag to the wait CLI args
- Add server-side support: subscribe to events or poll agent state
- The wait command already has timeout infrastructure to reuse
close_reason: Already released

title: botty-snapshot tmux alias should use --raw
description: The botty-snapshot tmux command alias (used in display-popup) should use --raw flag for cleaner output in the popup.
close_reason: Added --raw to botty-snapshot tmux alias.

title: botty view shows blank screen for Claude agents
description:
When running 'botty view' while Claude CLI agents are running, the tmux panes show blank screens instead of the agent output.

Reproduction:
1. Spawn a Claude agent: botty spawn --name test -- claude -p 'hello'
2. Run botty view
3. Observe blank pane instead of Claude's output

Likely cause: Claude CLI may use alternate screen buffer or special terminal handling that doesn't work well with the tail --replay approach.

Reported during cr-41e6 review when security-reviewer agent was running.
close_reason: Not a bug - claude -p outputs text and exits, doesn't launch TUI. View works correctly.

title: Fix clippy: replace unwrap() with expect()
description: Replace ~7 uses of unwrap() with expect() to provide better panic messages
close_reason: Fixed all clippy lints

title: Fix clippy: miscellaneous lints
description: Fix remaining clippy lints: option_if_let_else, doc_markdown backticks, match_same_arms, ignored_unit_patterns, items_after_statements, significant_drop_tightening, cast_sign_loss, cast_possible_truncation, too_many_lines
close_reason: Fixed all clippy lints

title: Detach sequence handling (Ctrl+A d)
description: Implement detach key sequence: Ctrl+A followed by 'd'. Restore user terminal to normal mode. Return cleanly to shell.
close_reason: Implemented attach mode with Ctrl+A d detach sequence and readonly option

title: Generate test scripts from recorded sessions
description:
Convert recorded command sessions into executable test scripts.

## Goal
Take the JSON recording from bd-3r3 and generate a runnable bash test script.

## Implementation
- New command: `botty gen-test <agent-id> > test.sh`
- Reads recording from server
- Outputs bash script with:
  - Spawn command
  - All send/send-keys with timing delays
  - Snapshot assertions at key points
  - Cleanup (kill agent)

## Example output:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e
AGENT=$(botty spawn -- bash)
sleep 0.5
botty send-keys $AGENT e c h o space h i enter
sleep 0.3
botty snapshot $AGENT | grep -q 'hi'
botty kill $AGENT
```

## Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `botty gen-test <agent-id>` outputs valid bash script
- [ ] Generated script is executable and passes
- [ ] Timing delays are reasonable (not too fast/slow)
- [ ] Script includes comments explaining what's being tested

## Dependencies
Depends on: bd-3r3 (need recording infrastructure first)

Part of: bd-2lj breakdown
close_reason: Merged. Added gen-test command with shell escaping, timing delays, and 18 unit tests.

title: Add kill --all flag
description:
Currently there's no way to kill all PTYs at once. Would be useful for:

- Quick cleanup during development
- Resetting state without restarting server
- Scripts that need to ensure clean slate

Implementation:
- Add `--all` flag to kill command
- Iterate through all agents and kill each
- Consider whether to require confirmation for safety
close_reason: Implemented kill --all flag

title: Add 'botty exec' convenience command (spawn + send + wait + output)
description: Single command for the common pattern: spawn agent, run command, wait for completion, return output. Example: botty exec -- ls -la. Returns just the command output, not full screen.
close_reason: Implemented in commit a93438c

title: Add --env flag to spawn command
description: Allow setting environment variables for spawned agents with --env KEY=VALUE (repeatable). Also add --env-clear to start with empty environment.
close_reason: Implemented --env KEY=VALUE (repeatable) and --env-clear flags for spawn command

title: Add --json as hidden alias for --format json
description: Agents frequently guess --json before discovering --format json (e.g. botty list --json). Add --json as a hidden alias (accepted silently, not shown in --help). This is now a standardized convention in the botbox CLI design guidelines.
close_reason: Implemented in v0.12.2. Added --json as hidden alias for --format json on spawn, list, kill, send, send-bytes, send-keys, and recording commands.

title: Implement list command
description: Return list of agents with id, pid, state, command, uptime. Format nicely for CLI output.
close_reason: Implemented list command showing id, pid, state, command

title: Add snapshot diff command
description:
Add command to show only what changed between snapshots, making test verification easier.

## Problem
When testing, often want to verify 'this one thing changed and nothing else'.
Currently requires:
- Taking snapshot
- Comparing manually or with external diff
- Visual inspection of full output

## Proposed solution
Option 1: New subcommand
```bash
botty snapshot-diff <agent-id> <previous-snapshot-file>
```

Option 2: Flag on snapshot
```bash
botty snapshot <agent-id> --diff <previous-snapshot-file>
```

## Output
Unified diff format showing what changed, or 'No changes' if identical.

## Implementation
- Use existing snapshot infrastructure
- Add diff library (e.g., similar_asserts, diff crate)
- ~1-2 hours of work

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `botty snapshot <id> --diff <file>` shows unified diff
- [ ] Exit code 0 if snapshots identical, 1 if different
- [ ] Output is colorized (if terminal supports it)
- [ ] Works with both regular and --raw snapshot formats
- [ ] Clear message if previous snapshot file doesn't exist

## Concerns/Notes
- May have false positives from timing indicators or cursor position
- Could also just use external diff, but built-in is more convenient
- Upgrading to P2 since it's very easy and immediately useful

## Labels
testing, cli
close_reason: Completed in commit eaa31d26. Added --diff flag to snapshot command to show unified diff between snapshots. Exit code 0 if identical, 1 if different.

title: Add basic command recording to agents
description:
First step toward test script generation: add ability to record commands sent to an agent.

## Goal
Track all send/send-keys/snapshot commands issued to a specific agent during its lifetime.

## Implementation
- Add recording flag to Agent struct in server
- When recording enabled, log commands to in-memory buffer
- Provide Request::GetRecording to retrieve the log

## Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `botty spawn --record -- bash` enables recording for that agent
- [ ] All send/send-keys commands are captured with timestamps
- [ ] Can retrieve recording via new command: `botty recording <agent-id>`
- [ ] Output is structured (JSON) for later script generation

## Non-goals
- Script generation (that's bd-2lj-gen)
- Replay functionality (that's bd-2lj-replay)
- Output formatting besides JSON

Blocks: bd-2lj-gen (script generation needs this)
Part of: bd-2lj breakdown
close_reason: Merged. Adds --record flag to spawn and botty recording command.

title: Trigger resize when transitioning from placeholder to active pane
description:
When transitioning from the 'Waiting for agents...' placeholder to an active pane, the new pane doesn't take up the full window.

Should trigger a resize or retile when replacing the placeholder with a real agent pane.
close_reason: Event loop now retiles after transitioning from placeholder to first real pane.

title: botty view: tmux/zellij integration
description: Launch a tmux session named 'botty' that shows all agents, one pane per agent (tiled layout). Auto-adapts as agents spawn/exit via event streaming. Each pane runs 'botty tail --replay <id>'. v0: tmux only.
close_reason: Implemented botty view with tmux integration

title: Add --format toon|json|text to structured output commands
description: All commands with structured output should default to TOON format and use --format toon|json|text instead of --json flag. Text format is optional per subcommand.
close_reason: Added --format toon|json|text to list command. TOON remains default. --json flag preserved as hidden deprecated alias.

title: view: detaching from tmux with no agents should shut down server and kill session
description:
When detaching from the botty tmux view session with no agents running, the server and tmux session should be cleaned up automatically. Currently this cleanup doesn't happen — the server keeps running and the tmux session persists.

NOT a regression from --proc changes — git history shows this cleanup was never implemented. The post-detach code in run_view_command() (main.rs ~line 1384) only unbinds Ctrl+P and aborts the event loop. It never checks for running agents or performs cleanup.

Implementation notes:
- After view.attach() returns (user detached), query the server for running agents
- If no running agents: send Request::Shutdown and kill the tmux session
- The TmuxView is moved into spawn_blocking, so either clone the session name beforehand or use tmux kill-session directly
- If agents ARE running, leave everything alone (user can reattach later)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Detaching from botty view with 0 running agents shuts down the server
- [ ] The tmux session is killed on detach when no agents are running
- [ ] Normal detach with running agents still works as before (no shutdown)
- [ ] Reattach after detach-with-agents still works
close_reason: Completed in commit 6cbd44d8. Implemented auto-cleanup: when detaching from view with no running agents, server shuts down and tmux session is killed. With agents running, server and session remain active for reattach. Manual testing required (documented in commit).

title: Fix clippy: add missing # Errors doc sections
description: Add # Errors documentation sections to ~24 functions returning Result
close_reason: Fixed all clippy lints

title: Implement wait_for_stable screen detection
description: Poll snapshot until screen hasn't changed for N milliseconds. Useful for waiting for TUI to finish rendering.
close_reason: Implemented TestHarness, AgentHandle, wait_for_content, wait_for_pattern, and wait_for_stable in testing module

title: Fix clippy: inline format args
description: Replace format!("{}", var) with format!("{var}") in ~15 places
close_reason: Fixed all clippy lints

title: Agent labels/groups
description: Add --label flag to spawn. Support 'botty kill --label X', 'botty list --label X', 'botty wait --any --label X --pattern Y'. Makes coordinating N agents tractable.
close_reason: Implemented agent labels/groups feature

title: Add botty doctor command
description: Diagnostic command to check: socket path writable, daemon running/startable, PTY allocation works, no stale sockets.
close_reason: Implemented botty doctor command with checks for socket, PTY, daemon, and spawn/kill cycle

title: botty view tmux pane title shows previous agent command after all closed
description:
When all agents are closed/killed, the tmux pane title in `botty view` still shows the last agent's command (e.g., "bash" or "htop") instead of indicating no agents are running.

## Expected behavior
When no agents are running, the pane title should show "Waiting for agents" or similar idle indicator.

## Reproduction
1. `botty view`
2. `botty spawn -- bash`
3. `botty kill <agent-id>`
4. Observe: pane title still shows "bash"

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] When all agents exit, pane title updates to an idle message (e.g., "No agents" or "Waiting for agents")
- [ ] When a new agent spawns, pane title updates to show the new agent

## Testing
- Spawn an agent, kill it, verify pane title changes
- Spawn multiple agents, kill all, verify title updates
- Check with `tmux display -p "#{pane_title}"` in the botty session
close_reason: Merged. Clears agent metadata from pane border when all agents exit.

comments.author: botty-dev
comments.text: Groomed: expanded description with reproduction steps, added acceptance criteria with concrete idle message expectation, added testing strategy

title: Strip ANSI codes in tail output by default
description: tail currently shows raw ANSI escape codes which is confusing. Should strip by default like snapshot does. Add --raw flag to preserve them if needed.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 284ff65

title: Kill command should handle already-exited agents gracefully
description: When running 'botty kill' on an agent that has already exited, it returns an error 'ESRCH: No such process'. Should check agent state first and return a friendlier message like 'agent already exited' or just succeed silently.
close_reason: Check agent state before sending signal, return Ok if already exited

title: Spawn dependencies: --after and --wait-for
description: 'botty spawn --after <id>' waits for agent to exit. 'botty spawn --wait-for <id> --pattern ready' waits for pattern. Declarative workflow sequencing.
close_reason: Implemented --after and --wait-for flags for spawn dependencies

title: Screen normalization (strip colors, regex filters)
description: Implement normalization options: strip ANSI, trim trailing whitespace, optional regex filters for volatile content (timestamps, PIDs).
close_reason: Implemented - snapshot already strips colors by default, --raw preserves them

title: Add 'botty wait' command for polling until content appears
description: CLI command to wait until snapshot contains a pattern. Example: botty wait <id> --contains 'Done' --timeout 30s. Essential for orchestration without manual sleeps.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 5b6b3b1

title: Implement kill command
description: Send signal to agent process (SIGTERM default, SIGKILL for --force). Update agent state. Clean up PTY resources.
close_reason: Implemented kill command with SIGTERM default and --force for SIGKILL

title: Resource limits: --timeout and --max-output
description:
Auto-kill agents after timeout. Cap transcript size. Prevent runaway agents from eating resources.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `--timeout <secs>` flag on spawn: auto-kill agent after N seconds
- [ ] `--max-output <bytes>` flag on spawn: stop recording transcript after limit
- [ ] Timeout triggers SIGTERM first, then SIGKILL after grace period
- [ ] Limits visible in `botty list --json` output
- [ ] Exit reason distinguishes timeout vs normal exit
- [ ] Unit tests for timeout and output limit behavior

Post-implementation:
- [ ] Create code review via `crit reviews create`
- [ ] Request security reviewer and architecture reviewer
- [ ] Spawn sub-agents to perform reviews using botty
- [ ] Address any review feedback
- [ ] Write brief report on the crit experiment
- [ ] File any crit issues as beads in ~/src/botcrit
close_reason: Implemented with --timeout and --max-output flags. Security review feedback addressed (exit reason race condition fix, timing precision documented).

title: Add pane banner showing name, processes, and labels
description:
Add a visual banner/header to each pane in botty view showing:
- Agent name/ID
- Running process(es)
- Labels if any

Could use tmux pane-border-format or a custom status line approach.
close_reason: Added pane-border-status with agent ID, command, and labels. Uses @agent_command and @agent_labels pane options set during pane creation and from spawn events.

title: View mode: panes vs windows
description: Add '--mode=<panes|windows>' flag to 'botty view'. Panes (default) shows all agents in split panes in one window. Windows mode creates a separate tmux window per agent, allowing tab-style navigation.
close_reason: Added --mode=panes|windows flag to botty view

title: Improve botty kill exit handling
description:
botty kill exits non-zero when agent already exited, requiring '|| true' in scripts.

Options:
1. Add --if-running flag: only kill if agent exists, no-op otherwise
2. Add wait-exit command: wait for clean exit with timeout
3. Make kill idempotent (exit 0 if agent not found)

Example:
  botty kill --if-running test-agent
  botty wait-exit test-agent --timeout 5

Makes cleanup in test scripts cleaner.

## Triage Notes (2026-01-29)
**Complexity**: Low - main.rs:373-397, just change error handling
**Recommendation**: Option 2 (idempotent) - exit 0 if "agent not found", still error on other failures
**Value**: Common cleanup pattern, matches Unix tools (rm -f, pkill)
**Implementation**: ~1 hour
**Upgrade**: P3→P2 (quick win, common pain point)
close_reason: Implemented in commit 5a27637. The kill command now exits with code 0 when agent not found, matching Unix tool behavior (rm -f, pkill). Added comprehensive CLI test to verify idempotent behavior. All tests pass.

title: Add timeout to placeholder infinite loop
description:
From security review cr-klwd (th-gu0z):

The placeholder command runs 'while true; do sleep 1; done' which runs forever if view is abandoned. Consider adding a maximum lifetime or heartbeat-based self-termination.
close_reason: Placeholder now uses sleep 3600 (1-hour timeout) instead of infinite loop.

title: Remove toon_format dependency
description:
After all commands are migrated to the new OutputFormat system, remove the toon_format crate.

## Changes
- Remove toon_format from Cargo.toml dependencies
- Remove any remaining toon-related code
- Verify no references remain

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] toon_format not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] No toon references in source code
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass

## Testing
- cargo build && cargo test
close_reason: Merged. Removed toon-format from Cargo.toml.

title: PTY module (openpty, fork, exec helpers)
description: Create PTY wrapper using nix crate: spawn_pty() that creates PTY pair, forks, sets up session/controlling terminal, execs command. Handle SIGCHLD for child reaping.
close_reason: Implemented PTY spawn with fork/exec, signal handling, and tests

title: Add --replay flag to tail command
description: When tailing with --replay, first output the current screen snapshot (via snapshot --raw), then stream updates. Essential for TUI viewing - without it, joining mid-session shows only incremental updates.
close_reason: Implemented --replay flag for tail command

title: Add exponential backoff to server retry loop
description:
From security review cr-klwd (th-385w):

The retry loop for server connection runs 50 iterations with 100ms fixed delay. Could add:
1. Exponential backoff
2. Check if spawned server process is still alive
3. Log connection errors for debugging
close_reason: Server retry loop now uses exponential backoff: 50ms → 500ms cap, 20 iterations max (~7.5s total).

title: Transcript ring buffer implementation
description: Per-agent ring buffer for raw PTY output. Fixed size (default 1MB). Store bytes + timestamps. Support byte-range and time-range queries.
close_reason: Implemented Transcript struct with ring buffer, append, tail_bytes, since, all methods

title: Create AgentHandle for fluent agent interaction
description: Wrapper around agent ID with methods like send(), snapshot(), wait_for_content(), wait_for_stable(), kill(). Provides ergonomic API for tests.
close_reason: Implemented TestHarness, AgentHandle, wait_for_content, wait_for_pattern, and wait_for_stable in testing module

title: Set minimal essential env vars for spawned agents
description: After clearing env on spawn (security hardening), agents now start with zero env vars. Add a minimal, hardcoded set of essential vars (PATH, HOME, USER, TERM, SHELL, LANG) that are always set from the server's env. Explicit --env overrides these. Document the set in --help for spawn.
close_reason: Implemented: spawned agents always get PATH, HOME, USER, TERM, SHELL, LANG from server env. Explicit --env overrides these. Documented in spawn --help.

title: AgentManager (owns agents, handles requests)
description: Create AgentManager: HashMap of agents, methods for spawn/list/kill/get. Handle concurrent access. Track agent lifecycle events.
close_reason: Implemented AgentManager with add/get/list/remove, name generation with collision handling

title: Add OutputFormat module with TTY auto-detection
description:
Create a shared output format module that all commands can use.

## Implementation
- Add `src/output.rs` with `OutputFormat` enum: `Text`, `Json`, `Pretty`
- Resolution order: `--format` flag > `FORMAT` env var > TTY→Pretty / non-TTY→Text
- Helper function: `resolve_format(flag: Option<&str>) -> OutputFormat`
- JSON envelope helper: `fn json_envelope(key: &str, data: Value, advice: Vec<String>) -> Value`
- Re-export from lib.rs

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] OutputFormat enum with Text/Json/Pretty variants
- [ ] resolve_format() respects flag > env > TTY detection
- [ ] json_envelope() wraps data in `{ <key>: data, "advice": [...] }`
- [ ] Unit tests for resolution logic and envelope

## Testing
- Unit tests for format resolution with various combinations
- Unit tests for JSON envelope structure
close_reason: Merged. OutputFormat module with TTY auto-detection, JSON envelope helper, text_record helper, 14 tests.
